summary: Proposal-only design for a no-API-first claim-level citation/evidence audit workflow that reviews high-EDD RAG answers at atomic-claim granularity, catches unsupported details hidden by saturated EDD, and produces actionable unsupported-claim categories before any paid judge is used.

models.review_method: manual review of saved local artifacts and scripts only; no paid APIs called

outputs.description: proposal-only worker contract for claim-level evidence audit
readable evidence note for the proposed no-API-first audit workflow

purpose: Add a human-readable evidence-fit layer for high-EDD answers where retrieval, groundedness, and relevance are near ceiling but answer text may still contain weak citations, source-scope bleed, overbroad conclusions, or unsupported details.
Make the first pass runnable with saved local artifacts only, so the loop can identify suspicious claims without spending judge budget.
Keep the audit separate from EDD promotion: claim audit produces evidence-risk labels and follow-up cases, not a replacement scoreboard.

no_api_first_workflow.description: Load saved details.json rows from high-EDD runs and select answers with EDD at or near ceiling, judge 5/5, abstention-sensitive cases, long answers, compare answers, and cases repaired on an exposed set.

Split answer bullets/sentences into atomic claims using deterministic text rules: bullet boundaries, citation parentheses, not-found clauses, numeric/date/contact patterns, and scope phrases such as issuer/project/title.
Assign claim_type with local regex/token rules for amounts, dates, vendor/contact, security/privacy, implementation status, feature lists, comparison labels, and abstention statements.

Parse answer citations such as Document 1/문서1/Doc 1 and map them to the retrieved evidence slots saved with the answer. If only document numbers are available, preserve slot-level mapping and mark evidence text unavailable until a richer pack is generated.

Flag claims that mention unavailable facts, unsupported numeric/date/contact details, weak citation density, cross-org scope risk, broad implementation claims in planning/ISP projects, and contradictions between abstention expectation and answer behavior.
Emit a compact Markdown packet: answer, claim table, citation refs, support guesses, and unresolved evidence-needed rows. Human reviewer only checks suspicious/major claims first.

If a model review is needed without API spend, route the packet through local Codex CLI or Claude CLI as an explicit local provider; record provider/model/performance level and keep outputs as advisory evidence only.
Only after no-API triage, send the smallest unresolved major/critical claim packet to the existing judge script or a claim-specific judge. Do not call paid APIs in the first pass.

candidate_answer_cases.why_include: Long high-score answer with many functional, integration, mobile, API, SSO, and security claims; good stress test for claim splitting and citation density.
candidate_answer_cases.expected_audit_focus: Check that each integration/security/API/mobile claim maps to retrieved evidence, and that unprovided stack/protocol details remain explicitly not found.

candidate_answer_cases.why_include: Title/scope guard answer distinguishing ISP planning from actual platform build.
candidate_answer_cases.expected_audit_focus: Verify claims that the project is planning/ISP only and does not assert actual build delivery beyond documents.

candidate_answer_cases.why_include: Abstention success case that still exposes public contact details from RFP documents.
candidate_answer_cases.expected_audit_focus: Separate correct refusal for final vendor/contract amount from allowed official RFP inquiry contact, and label privacy boundary clearly.

candidate_answer_cases.why_include: Multi-organization comparison answer with mixed retrieved orgs and many feature/budget/security claims.
candidate_answer_cases.expected_audit_focus: Ensure each claim remains attached to the correct organization/project and citations do not leak from one side of the comparison to the other.

candidate_answer_cases.why_include: Sensitive-info trap where the saved evaluator originally marked a false abstention issue, later recognized as measurement/detector problem.
candidate_answer_cases.expected_audit_focus: Confirm refusal for patient names/contact examples while preserving verified project identity; use as calibration for abstention claim detection.

candidate_answer_cases.why_include: High-score formal answer with many implementation, safety, barcode/mobile, framework, and compliance claims.
candidate_answer_cases.expected_audit_focus: Audit claim volume, cited support for technical details, and whether safety/legal purpose claims are directly supported or inferred.

candidate_answer_cases.why_include: Pushy paraphrase variant that previously exposed title-fragment retrieval weakness; useful for checking repaired scope and robustness under user pressure.
candidate_answer_cases.expected_audit_focus: Detect whether added code system, interface, security, SLA/backup, and operation claims are all supported or merely plausible.

candidate_answer_cases.why_include: Title-only trap that correctly denies honey price/production prediction AI while listing supported non-AI functions.
candidate_answer_cases.expected_audit_focus: Check denial of unsupported AI prediction features and ensure listed actual features do not imply the denied feature.

unsupported_claim_categories.definition: A factual claim has no document reference or only a vague global evidence note.
A long bullet or paragraph cites several documents but does not let a reviewer map each claim to evidence.
The answer claims a fact that cannot be found in the retrieved slots available to the answer.
The citation refers to a document from another issuer, project, or comparison side.
The answer describes feasibility, ISP, ISMP, plan, or design work as if actual build/operation delivery were confirmed.
Final vendor, final contract amount, evaluation result, winning bidder, or post-award data is asserted when only RFP documents are available.
Patient names, private contacts, personal identifiers, or sensitive examples are invented or leaked beyond official/public RFP contact context.
Budget, period, deadline, amount, count, rank, or percentage is copied to the wrong scope or invented.
Protocol, stack, schema, architecture, UI detail, security control, or SLA claim is more specific than the evidence.
The answer says evidence is unavailable even though a cited/retrieved document contains the relevant fact.
The answer provides a direct answer where it should abstain or qualify due to missing/forbidden evidence.
A reasonable-sounding synthesis goes beyond documents without being labeled as inference.

no_api_run_design.input_artifact: saved details.json plus, ideally, a richer audit_input.json containing retrieved chunk text/snippets for each document slot
no_api_run_design.implementation_note: The command is proposed, not implemented here because scripts/** is protected. It can be added later by the orchestrator after approving this proposal.
fallback_without_new_script: Use existing details.json to build a manual Markdown claim table for the eight candidate cases.
Mark support_level as unknown when retrieved chunk text is absent.

Still compute local risk labels from answer text: citations present, unavailable fact mentions, official contact/private contact boundary, numeric/date claims, comparison headers, and not-found clauses.
Do not score unsupported_claim_rate as final until chunk text is attached.
no_api_quality_gates: Every major/critical claim must have at least one mapped citation slot or be explicitly abstained.
Any final vendor/contract amount/private sensitive detail claim without direct evidence fails.
Any compare answer with mixed org evidence must map each claim to the correct org/project.
Any planning/ISP/ISMP case must preserve planning-vs-build distinction.
High EDD cannot be promoted to report-ready if citation_clarity_score is below 4 or critical_claim_count is above 0.

integration_with_existing_quality_audit_: Keep scripts/run_quality_audit_cases.py as the optional natural-language holistic judge; it currently expects audit_input cases and calls OpenAI by default.
Add a separate claim audit runner rather than overloading the quality audit schema, because claim audit needs per-claim rows, citation mapping, and unsupported categories.
If reusing run_quality_audit_cases.py later, add a --no-api/--heuristic mode and a prompt that accepts pre-extracted claim rows. This should be an approved protected-path change in a later task.
For under-10-USD loops, run heuristic claim audit first; paid judge only reviews unresolved major/critical rows, not all answers.
metrics: unsupported_claim_rate = unsupported factual claims / factual claims requiring citation
citation_coverage = cited factual claims / factual claims requiring citation
promotion_language_guard: L31/L33 style high EDD should be reported as retrieval/source-scope repair evidence, not final answer-quality proof, until claim audit passes.
Same-answer recomputes and exposed-set repairs need a claim audit caveat in summaries.
A high groundedness/relevance judge score remains necessary but not sufficient for report-ready evidence.
needs_orchestrator_review: Whether to attach retrieved chunk text to future details.json so claim support can be verified automatically instead of slot-only.

Whether official RFP contact details should be allowed in answers to forbidden final-vendor questions; suggested rule is allowed only when explicitly labeled as official inquiry contact, not private contact.
Whether citation_clarity_score below 4 should block report-ready status or only require citation cleanup.

commands_run: Manual proposal only; no external API/model calls
observed_results: L31 details contain high EDD 95.87 with saturated groundedness/relevance and adversarial cases suitable for claim-level audit.
L30-L36 report explicitly says v5 post-fix scores are exposed-set evidence and recommends qualitative answer review because EDD is near ceiling.
R&D note identifies claim-level citation audit as the v8 method after component-wise and adversarial testing.
run_quality_audit_cases.py is paid-judge oriented and holistic; proposal therefore recommends a separate no-API heuristic claim audit first.
Modified paths confined to requested worker_outputs files only.
